checks.finding: REQ-001..REQ-006 in requirements.md each specify concrete, mechanically checkable artifacts (schema validation, grep-based drift checks, negative self-checks, RED-differential against 2d8c6a5^, document/CHANGELOG conformance). OQ-5 (task-review cross-stage semantics) is explicitly flagged open but bounded ('the task-review leg encodes only the behavior observable at HEAD') with a named resolution owner (A3 implementation report), so it does not leave an in-scope requirement untestable.

Every goal REQ-001..REQ-006 has explicit '(REQ-NNN)' back-references from one or more of AC-001..AC-016 in acceptance-tests.md (e.g., REQ-001->AC-001..004, REQ-003->AC-008..010, REQ-006->AC-016); no goal is left without a matching acceptance criterion.

AC-001..AC-016 describe externally observable outcomes (specific file/schema validation, grep results, suite pass/fail, SKIP-with-reason naming, red/green suite states) rather than vague intent; e.g. AC-002 requires a negative self-check that 'mutates a cap in a temp copy and asserts red', and AC-013 requires the exact 'deterministic-runtime-unavailable' output as a named SKIP.

Non-goals section explicitly excludes re-fixing #143, modifying protected gate/test files, A5-A7/Pillar B work (#145-#148), building the #125 harness itself, and tasks.md/traceability.md; Field Definitions further closes the fixture_profiles vocabulary to greenfield/brownfield, preventing unbounded expansion.

Security Boundaries table (B1-B3) states auth/authz, data-classification, and regulatory constraints (mktemp isolation, real-gate read-only driving, no protected-basename+write-verb on command lines); Roles and Permissions states the human-approval boundary (spec/task approval, RED-differential review, OQ-5 sign-off); Non-goals states the protected-gate compatibility constraint.

Risks section lists Critical/Critical/High/Medium/Medium risks, each with a mitigation tied to a concrete validation surface: cap-drift and false-green risks tie to AC-001/002/007/010/012 negative self-checks; the CI-runtime-cost risk ties to a described future 'runtime measurement recorded at implementation with a budget assertion'; the RED-differential ancestry risk and brownfield-seed-timing risk tie to AC-009 and AC-005 respectively.
